description: This figure presents experimental and simulated dynamic recrystallization (DRX) microstructure evolution of low-alloy steel under constant and decreasing temperature deformation at 0.01 s⁻¹. Subfigure (a) shows EBSD reconstructed prior austenite microstructures and corresponding GOS maps at 0.9 strain for samples deformed at constant 950°C, and decreasing temperature from 1050°C to 950°C with εI=0.2 and 0.4, with DRX fractions of 97.2%, 96.1%, 97.2% and average grain sizes of 52.01±3.48μm, 53.29±3.77μm, 60.93±2.91μm respectively. Subfigure (b) compares reconstructed microstructures at 0.4 strain (1050°C deformation), 0.5 strain (after temperature drop) and 0.5 strain under constant 950°C, with average grain sizes of 80.79±5.43μm, 74.40±5.36μm, 56.97±5.24μm. Subfigures (c) and (d) show 3D cellular automaton simulated microstructure evolution under constant 950°C and decreasing temperature conditions respectively, with DRX fractions and average grain sizes marked for each strain level. White grains represent matrix, green grains are first-stage DRX grains formed at 1050°C_0.01s⁻¹, red grains are second-stage DRX grains formed at 950°C_0.01s⁻¹. Simulated results agree well with experimental EBSD observations, validating the accuracy of the 3D CA model for predicting multi-stage DRX behavior under varying temperature deformation.
